status_notes: Initial research from AWS CloudWatch documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live AWS environment).
notes: Foundation monitoring service. Metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards for all AWS services.

match_nodes.description: Built-in and custom metrics for all AWS services

data.description: Time-series data points published by AWS services and custom applications. AWS services automatically publish metrics (EC2 CPU utilization, S3 request counts, RDS connections, etc.). Custom metrics can be published via PutMetricData API. Metrics are organized by namespace, metric name, and dimensions.

options.description: Available for EC2, RDS, and other services

default.note: Most AWS services publish basic metrics at no additional charge. Detailed monitoring requires explicit enablement.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable detailed monitoring for production EC2 instances and RDS databases. Use custom metrics for application-specific monitoring. Use metric math for derived metrics without publishing redundant data.

audit.cli_data: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value={id} --start-time {start} --end-time {end} --period 300 --statistics Average

data.description: Monitor metrics and trigger actions when thresholds are crossed. Three states: OK (metric within threshold), ALARM (metric crossed threshold), INSUFFICIENT_DATA (not enough data). Actions include SNS notifications, Auto Scaling policies, EC2 actions (stop, terminate, reboot, recover), and Systems Manager OpsItems.

options.description: Single metric threshold with configurable evaluation periods
Combines multiple alarms with AND/OR logic to reduce alarm noise
ML-based expected value band — alarms when metric deviates from learned pattern

data.vendor_recommendation: Create alarms for all critical security and operational metrics. Use composite alarms to reduce noise. Set up SNS topics to route to email, Slack, PagerDuty, or incident management systems.

configure.cli: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name {name} --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value={id} --statistic Average --period 300 --evaluation-periods 2 --threshold 90 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --alarm-actions {sns-arn}

data.description: Centralized log management service. Logs are organized into log groups (retention container) and log streams (sequence of events from one source). Supports ingestion from AWS services (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, Lambda, ECS), EC2 instances (via CloudWatch Agent), and custom applications.

options.description: Infrequent Access is lower cost for logs queried infrequently

default.note: Log groups retain logs indefinitely by default. Set retention policies to control costs.

data.vendor_recommendation: Set explicit retention policies on all log groups to control costs. Use Infrequent Access log class for high-volume logs that are rarely queried. Route security-critical logs to S3 for long-term archival.

data.description: Define patterns to search for in log data and create CloudWatch metrics from matching entries. Used for alerting on specific log events. Common security use cases: root account login, unauthorized API calls, IAM policy changes, console sign-in without MFA, security group changes, NACL changes, CloudTrail configuration changes.
data.vendor_recommendation: Create metric filters for all CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark recommended log metrics. Pair each metric filter with a CloudWatch alarm and SNS notification. Key filters: root login, unauthorized API calls, console sign-in failures, IAM changes, CloudTrail changes, S3 bucket policy changes, config changes.

audit.console: CloudWatch → Logs → Log groups → {group} → Metric filters

match_nodes.description: Custom visualization of metrics and logs

data.description: Customizable web pages in the CloudWatch console for monitoring resources. Support multiple widget types (line graphs, stacked area, number, text, logs, alarms). Cross-account and cross-region dashboards enable centralized monitoring. Dashboards can be shared via direct links or embedded in other applications.

options.description: Multiple metrics shown as stacked areas
Metric value shown as a gauge

data.description: Machine learning models that analyze metric history to generate expected value bands. Models learn daily, weekly, and seasonal patterns. When a metric value falls outside the expected band, it indicates an anomaly. Can be used as the basis for CloudWatch alarms.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable anomaly detection for security-relevant metrics where static thresholds are insufficient (e.g., API call volumes, network traffic patterns, login attempt rates). Combine with alarms for automated alerting on unexpected deviations.

audit.console: CloudWatch → Metrics → {metric} → Graphed metrics → Anomaly detection band
